title: Test/runtime contract drift scan
description: Find tests, docs/test-seed entries, snapshots, and generated fixtures that encode retired or mismatched runtime contracts. Compare test artifacts against current implementation and public docs. Report only concrete drift with file:line evidence.
findings: Found 1 fresh medium-severity test/runtime contract drift in executable dogfood harnesses; shared discovery board was appended with the cross-cutting pattern.
issues_found: [{"title":"Dogfood harnesses still call retired extract/review contracts","severity":"medium","description":"scripts/dogfood-rc2-archive.mjs still calls extractKnowledge with legacy source_session, while the current MCP/schema contract requires non-empty source_sessions[] and the tool handler re-parses to reject missing arrays (packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts:646; packages/server/src/tools/extract-knowledge.ts:49). Related dogfood review/doctor harnesses still hard-code project-local .fabric/knowledge pending/canonical paths (scripts/dogfood-rc3-review.mjs:62, scripts/dogfood-rc4-doctor.mjs:155), but current write-side code says knowledge writes are store-only with no dual-root fallback (packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:43). This is distinct from registered doc/test-seed drift because these are executable scripts that can silently produce empty-session idempotency keys or fail against store-only path validation when re-run.","location":"scripts/dogfood-rc2-archive.mjs:115","fix_direction":"Retire or quarantine the rc2/rc3/rc4 dogfood harnesses as historical evidence, or update them to use source_sessions: [SOURCE_SESSION], fab_review-returned store pending_path values, and resolved write-target store paths; add a small contract test or README note so legacy harnesses are not treated as current runtime validation.","affected_components":["scripts/dogfood-rc2-archive.mjs","scripts/dogfood-rc3-review.mjs","scripts/dogfood-rc4-doctor.mjs","fab_extract_knowledge contract","fab_review store-only review path"],"perspective":"test-runtime-contract-drift"}]

title: Workflow completeness gap scan
description: Find public flows whose tests cover only fragments rather than complete user-visible workflows: install/init/doctor/archive/review/store/sync/onboard/cite flows. Look for missing integration coverage, missing end-to-end assertions, or flows only protected by unit happy paths.
findings: Found one fresh workflow-completeness gap: onboard-coverage tests seed retired project-local knowledge and call only the helper, while the implementation scans mounted store read-sets.
issues_found: [{"title":"fabric onboard-coverage tests do not exercise the mounted-store user workflow","severity":"medium","description":"The public onboard-coverage command resolves the current project's mounted store read-set before scanning canonical knowledge (packages/cli/src/commands/onboard-coverage.ts:178 and :203), but its tests import only runOnboardCoverage and seed files under project-local .fabric/knowledge (packages/cli/__tests__/onboard-coverage.test.ts:23 and :51). The shared resolver/builders show the real workflow depends on global/project store config and cross-store reads (packages/shared/src/store/resolve-input.ts:20; packages/shared/src/store/core.ts:134), so the current suite covers parser/helper fragments rather than the user-visible mounted-store workflow. This is distinct from registered store-only docs/runtime drift because the issue is missing integration coverage for the command's true read-set path.","location":"packages/cli/__tests__/onboard-coverage.test.ts:51","fix_direction":"Replace or supplement the local .fabric/knowledge fixtures with an integration fixture that creates a global Fabric config, mounts a store with store.json, binds it in project fabric-config.json, writes canonical knowledge/<type> entries in that store, and invokes onboardCoverageCommand with --json/human output assertions.","affected_components":["packages/cli/src/commands/onboard-coverage.ts","packages/cli/__tests__/onboard-coverage.test.ts","packages/shared/src/store/resolve-input.ts","packages/shared/src/store/core.ts","fabric onboard-coverage workflow"],"perspective":"workflow-completeness-gaps"}]

title: Negative edge/error coverage scan
description: Find missing or weak coverage for error paths, invalid inputs, filesystem failures, malformed JSON/JSONL, collisions, stale state, concurrency, and graceful degradation. Report gaps where code has branches or contracts not exercised by tests.
findings: Found 2 fresh high-confidence negative/error coverage gaps in install-v2 pipeline stages; both have concrete code branches and no matching tests/registered duplicates.
issues_found: [{"title":"Install v2 HooksStage error results are untested and still reported as completed","severity":"high","description":"HooksStage catches step failures into InstallStepResult objects with status=\"error\" at packages/cli/src/install/pipeline/hooks.stage.ts:134 and :151, collects them into an errors array at :111, but then ignores that array and returns stageRan(\"hooks\") at :117. Registry search found no duplicate, and tests do not reference HooksStage; packages/cli/__tests__/install-v2-pipeline.test.ts covers only guidance/env/store happy paths, so no negative test proves a failing hook/config/bootstrap step makes the pipeline fail instead of showing install success.","location":"packages/cli/src/install/pipeline/hooks.stage.ts:117","fix_direction":"Add a HooksStage unit/integration test that forces one best-effort and one single-step installer to fail, then assert the stage or pipeline returns disposition='failed' with errors; change HooksStage to return stageFailedFromError or an explicit failed StageResult when installResults contains any status='error'.","affected_components":["packages/cli/src/install/pipeline/hooks.stage.ts","packages/cli/src/install/pipeline/pipeline.ts","packages/cli/__tests__/install-v2-pipeline.test.ts","install-v2 hooks/bootstrap install flow"],"perspective":"negative-edge-error-coverage"},{"title":"Install v2 PreflightStage writable and git negative paths are empty and uncovered","severity":"high","description":"PreflightStage documents and calls write-permission and git-availability checks at packages/cli/src/install/pipeline/preflight.stage.ts:40-45, but assertWritable and assertGitAvailable are empty methods at :93 and :98, while assertGlobalRootWritable only assumes an existing directory is writable at :87. Registry/test search found no PreflightStage, assertWritable, or assertGitAvailable tests, so unwritable targets/global roots and missing git for --url are not exercised before later stages perform writes or clone operations.","location":"packages/cli/src/install/pipeline/preflight.stage.ts:93","fix_direction":"Implement the preflight contracts with fs access/temp-file checks for target/global-root writability and a mocked spawn/exec git probe for --url, then add tests for unwritable target, global root as file/unwritable directory, no HOME/USERPROFILE, and missing git returning a failed preflight StageResult.","affected_components":["packages/cli/src/install/pipeline/preflight.stage.ts","packages/cli/src/install/pipeline/store.stage.ts","packages/cli/src/install/run-global-install.ts","packages/cli/__tests__/install-v2-pipeline.test.ts","install-v2 preflight"],"perspective":"negative-edge-error-coverage"}]

title: Fixture/snapshot stale protection scan
description: Find snapshot, fixture, seed, and golden-test practices that can pass while protecting stale behavior, old names, retired commands, or implementation noise instead of stable contracts. Include cases where snapshots need semantic assertions.
findings: Found 1 fresh fixture/snapshot stale-protection issue: the CLI i18n install snapshot pins stale Cursor hook/skill capability output despite current installer/parity evidence that Cursor hooks/bootstrap are delivered.
issues_found: [{"title":"CLI i18n snapshot pins stale Cursor hook/skill capability output","severity":"medium","description":"The install i18n snapshot locks the Cursor row as `hook=n/a`, `skill=n/a`, and `manual step required`, while the current installer writes Cursor hook config/bootstrap and the parity matrix explicitly says Cursor has full skill/hook/mcp support. The snapshot test only accepts the full sanitized stdout, so updating it with `vitest -u` can continue protecting the stale capability presentation instead of asserting the semantic contract that installed Cursor assets must be reflected in the capability summary. This is distinct from the registered Cursor docs drift: it is a test/fixture baseline that preserves an incorrect runtime capability display.","location":"packages/cli/__tests__/__snapshots__/i18n.test.ts.snap:47","fix_direction":"Replace the broad stdout snapshot for the capability table with semantic assertions over parsed rows, and fix `detectClientSupports` so Cursor reports hook/bootstrap assets after install; keep the snapshot only for locale copy once the row semantics are asserted.","affected_components":["packages/cli/__tests__/__snapshots__/i18n.test.ts.snap","packages/cli/__tests__/i18n.test.ts","packages/cli/src/config/resolver.ts","packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts","packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts","packages/shared/src/schemas/parity-matrix.ts"],"perspective":"fixture-snapshot-stale-protection"}]

title: TDD red authenticity and test quality scan
description: Find tests that are not meaningful red-first guards: skipped/only tests, tautological assertions, excessive mocking of the unit under test, brittle implementation assertions, missing fail-before-fix evidence, or broad coverage claims without assertions.
findings: Found 2 fresh test-quality issues: one unconditional skipped MCP initialize guard despite header coverage claims, and CLI/server coverage gates below the project 80% target.
issues_found: [{"title":"MCP HTTP initialize success path is claimed but unconditionally skipped","severity":"medium","description":"The HTTP integration test header claims coverage for the MCP HTTP transport initialize handshake, but the only test that asserts a successful initialize/session-creation response is an unconditional it.skip. The nearby 400/404 cases exercise non-initialize failure branches only, so the success path can regress while the suite remains green. This is distinct from existing ISS-20260608-042, which is about the retired /api/intent/annotate header entry, not this skipped MCP initialize guard.","location":"packages/server-http-experimental/__tests__/integration/http-endpoints.test.ts:327","fix_direction":"Replace the skipped test with a runnable isolated initialize handshake test by providing the required server/tool-registration environment or factoring createSession behind an injectable adapter; if the path remains intentionally out of scope, remove the header claim and add an explicit tracked TODO/issue instead of it.skip.","affected_components":["packages/server-http-experimental/__tests__/integration/http-endpoints.test.ts","MCP HTTP transport tests","createFabricHttpApp /mcp route"],"perspective":"tdd-red-authenticity-and-test-quality"},{"title":"CLI and server coverage gates are below the project target","severity":"low","description":"Root test:coverage delegates to package coverage scripts, but the CLI package accepts 70% lines/statements and the server package accepts 75%, below the project-level 80%+ testing standard. A broad coverage gate can therefore pass while two primary packages are under the expected minimum; shared already uses 85%, so this is not a repo-wide technical limitation.","location":"packages/cli/vitest.config.ts:35","fix_direction":"Raise CLI and server coverage thresholds to at least 80% lines/statements, or document a temporary exception with a burn-down target and package-specific missing-coverage list; keep the root coverage script as the enforcing entrypoint.","affected_components":["packages/cli/vitest.config.ts","packages/server/vitest.config.ts","package.json test:coverage"],"perspective":"tdd-red-authenticity-and-test-quality"}]

title: Dedup + Issue Creation
description: Aggregate all wave-1 findings. Deduplicate by file path + description similarity or same file:line, keep higher severity, assign collision-safe ISS-YYYYMMDD-NNN records, append source=discover issues to .workflow/issues/issues.jsonl and this session discovery-issues.jsonl.
findings: 7 upstream issues -> 7 after run dedup -> 7 created (ISS-20260609-005..011); 0 existing duplicates; 0 in-run duplicates. Adjacent store/docs/Cursor issues were not same title/location/root cause.
issues_found: [{"id":"ISS-20260609-005","title":"Dogfood harnesses still call retired extract/review contracts","severity":"medium","source":"discover","location":"scripts/dogfood-rc2-archive.mjs:115"},{"id":"ISS-20260609-006","title":"fabric onboard-coverage tests do not exercise the mounted-store user workflow","severity":"medium","source":"discover","location":"packages/cli/__tests__/onboard-coverage.test.ts:51"},{"id":"ISS-20260609-007","title":"Install v2 HooksStage error results are untested and still reported as completed","severity":"high","source":"discover","location":"packages/cli/src/install/pipeline/hooks.stage.ts:117"},{"id":"ISS-20260609-008","title":"Install v2 PreflightStage writable and git negative paths are empty and uncovered","severity":"high","source":"discover","location":"packages/cli/src/install/pipeline/preflight.stage.ts:93"},{"id":"ISS-20260609-009","title":"CLI i18n snapshot pins stale Cursor hook/skill capability output","severity":"medium","source":"discover","location":"packages/cli/__tests__/__snapshots__/i18n.test.ts.snap:47"},{"id":"ISS-20260609-010","title":"MCP HTTP initialize success path is claimed but unconditionally skipped","severity":"medium","source":"discover","location":"packages/server-http-experimental/__tests__/integration/http-endpoints.test.ts:327"},{"id":"ISS-20260609-011","title":"CLI and server coverage gates are below the project target","severity":"low","source":"discover","location":"packages/cli/vitest.config.ts:35"}]
